Overview

Address
23352 COURTHOUSE HIGHWAY, WINDSOR, VA 23487
Phone
7572424770
Certified beds

Avg daily residents
111 (97% of beds filled)
Ownership
For-profit corporation
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
2000-08-01
Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 4/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 5/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 3/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 2.61 3.34 3.02 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.29 0.58 0.34 ≥ 0.55 Below floor
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.74 0.74 0.86 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.57 2.01 1.82 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.03 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.02 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.21
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.21
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.56
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
49.4%
annual
RN turnover
72.7%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.18
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.86
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-05-01 20 20 2 100 1 100
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2021-03-26 20 15 5 88 1 88
Total weighted health score
97.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (62)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

62 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0677 Provide care and assistance to perform activities of daily living for any resident who is unable. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0678 Provide basic life support, including CPR, prior to the arrival of emergency medical personnel , subject to physician orders and the resident’s advance directives. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0687 Provide appropriate foot care. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0725 Provide enough nursing staff every day to meet the needs of every resident; and have a licensed nurse in charge on each shift. F 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0730 Observe each nurse aide's job performance and give regular training. E 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. E 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2025-05-01 2025-06-26
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2024-11-14 2025-01-04
0691 Provide appropriate colostomy, urostomy, or ileostomy care/services for a resident who requires such services. D 2024-11-14 2025-01-04
0692 Provide enough food/fluids to maintain a resident's health. D 2024-11-14 2025-01-04
0919 Make sure that a working call system is available in each resident's bathroom and bathing area. E 2024-11-14 2025-01-04
0925 Make sure there is a pest control program to prevent/deal with mice, insects, or other pests. E 2024-11-14 2025-01-04
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0567 Honor the resident's right to manage his or her financial affairs. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0574 The resident has the right to receive notices in a format and a language he or she understands. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0602 Protect each resident from the wrongful use of the resident's belongings or money. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0623 Provide timely notification to the resident, and if applicable to the resident representative and ombudsman, before transfer or discharge, including appeal rights. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0625 Notify the resident or the resident’s representative in writing how long the nursing home will hold the resident’s bed in cases of transfer to a hospital or therapeutic leave. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2021-03-26 2020-07-26
0690 Provide appropriate care for residents who are continent or incontinent of bowel/bladder, appropriate catheter care, and appropriate care to prevent urinary tract infections. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0773 Provide or obtain laboratory tests/services when ordered and promptly tell the ordering practitioner of the results. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0775 Keep complete, dated laboratory records in the resident's record. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2021-03-26 2021-05-07
0561 Honor the resident's right to and the facility must promote and facilitate resident self-determination through support of resident choice. E 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0582 Give residents notice of Medicaid/Medicare coverage and potential liability for services not covered. D 2019-06-27 2019-02-26
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. D 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2019-06-27 2019-08-06
0622 Not transfer or discharge a resident without an adequate reason; and must provide documentation and convey specific information when a resident is transferred or discharged. E 2019-06-27 2019-08-06

Deficiencies 0758 — description Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. 0758.description
